data.title: Wayfair bounces off its 52-week low after positive development with Vietnam tariff scenario
data.description:
Wayfair (NYSE:W) moved off its 52-week low from earlier in the session after President Trump said he had a productive call with Vietnam General Secretary of the Communist Party To Lam in regard to working out an agreement on tariffs.

Notably, the online retailer has higher exposure to Vietnam than some of its competitors. Wayfair (NYSE:W) said on Thursday that it is closely monitoring the evolving trade landscape, and sees itself as well-positioned to continue to offer customers the best possible combination of value, assortment, and experience.

Shares of Wayfair (W) were up 2.2% at 12:50 p.m. to cut into the weekly drop of more than 25% on above-average volume. At last check, Wayfair (W) shares were swapping hands at $25.64 vs. the 52-week range of $20.42 to $76.18. Short interest on Wayfair (W) stands at 17.0% of the total float, which could add to the volatility.

data.title: Hyundai won't raise vehicle prices until June
data.description:
Korea’s Hyundai Motors (OTCPK:HYMTF) will freeze the prices of its current model lineup until June to give consumers “some stability in the coming months.”

Although many of Hyundai (OTCPK:HYMTF) vehicles are made in the U.S. at Hyundai’s Motor Manufacturing Alabama (“HMMA”) facility in Montgomery, including popular models such as the Elantra, Sonata, Tucson, Santa Fe, and Santa Cruz, some of these models source as much as 90% of the parts and components from outside the U.S. including suppliers in South Korea, China, and Mexico.

As recently as Monday, the CEO of Hyundai and Genesis Motor North America warned dealers that “current vehicle pricing is not guaranteed and may be subject to change for units sold after April 2.”

To mitigate the damage from tariffs, Hyundai recently announced a $21B investment in the U.S.  

Hyundai (OTCPK:HYMTF) shares are lower for a seventh straight day.

data.title: SA Rating Changes: CHTR, RIVN, EE, WASH, DV, LZ, RXST
data.description:
Here's a roundup of the latest rating changes by Seeking Alpha analysts.

Upgrades:

Charter Communications (NASDAQ:CHTR). Upgraded to buy from hold by Seeking Profits. "With its cap-ex and cost-cutting plan progressing credibly and its domestic focus, CHTR shares can outperform during this period of uncertainty."

Rivian (NASDAQ:RIVN). Upgraded to strong buy from hold by Noah's Arc Capital. "Rivian's (NASDAQ:RIVN) U.S.-based manufacturing shields it from Trump's 25% tariffs on imported vehicles, creating a competitive pricing advantage over foreign EV and ICE vehicles…Despite potential supply chain challenges, Rivian's (RIVN) robust liquidity and favorable trade environment position it well to capitalize on market opportunities and scale production."

Downgrades: 

Excelerate Energy (NYSE:EE). Downgraded to sell from buy by Ronald Ferrie. "Investors are advised to take profits from EE stock due to management's poor capital allocation decisions and questionable decision-making, leading to a downgrade to SELL."

DoubleVerify (NYSE:DV). Downgraded to hold from buy by Eleceed Capital. "I am downgrading DoubleVerify to a hold rating due to worsening macroeconomic conditions and internal business challenges."

LegalZoom (NASDAQ:LZ). Downgraded to hold from buy by May Investing Ideas. "Tariffs and macro uncertainties are likely to hinder new business starts, posing a significant risk to LZ's growth outlook."

Washington Trust (NASDAQ:WASH). Downgraded to hold from buy by Eugenio Catone. "Despite a solid dividend yield of 8%, I don't foresee growth in the near term, and caution is advised due to recession risks."

RxSight (NASDAQ:RXST). Downgraded to hold from buy by Stephen Ayers. "Revised 2025 outlook and lack of expected profitability suggest earlier optimism was premature…Given economic uncertainties and RxSight’s speculative nature, I recommend a "Hold" rating until market trends and macroeconomic impacts become clearer."

data.title: Better Choice signs definitive agreement for SRx Health debt conversion
data.description:
• Better Choice (NYSE:BTTR) has announced that SRx Health Solutions has signed a definitive agreement to convert C$4M in debt to equity, improving SRx Health working capital position and future cash flow.
  • This strategic investment was made by a strategic distribution partner to invest in the Company's mission to deliver innovative, sustainable, and integrated healthcare solutions to patients in North America.
  • This investment is pending the closing of the merger between SRx Health Solutions and Better Choice Company, which is scheduled for late-April.
  • Upon closing, the combined companies will emerge as a leading global health and wellness company, delivering enhanced products and solutions for pets, people, and families.
  • BTTR is +10.85% to $1.84.

data.title: Artificial heart maker Picard Medical seeks U.S. IPO
data.description:
Picard Medical (PMI), the developer of the SynCardia artificial heart, disclosed this week its plans to seek a public listing on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol PMI to raise as much as $12M in net proceeds.

SynCardia, the only FDA-approved implantable total artificial heart, is sold and manufactured by the company’s 100% owned subsidiary, SynCardia Systems.

According to Tucson, Arizona-headquartered Picard (PMI), more than 2,100 patients across 27 countries have received SynCardia implants so far, and the company recorded $4.4M in revenue last year with a ~13% YoY drop.

In an SEC filing on Monday, the company said it intends to use net proceeds from the IPO to allocate $2.85M for its Chinese unit, SynCardia Medical (Beijing), and up to $5M for R&D work related to its implantable system.

Additionally, nearly $1M is earmarked for sales, marketing, and distribution related to SynCardia, and another $4M is allotted for general operational expenses, working capital, and other general corporate purposes.

data.title: Sotera Health to pay $30.9M in Illinois facility case settlement
data.description:
Sotera Health (NASDAQ: SHC) said that its Sterigenics unit will be paying a settlement of $30.9M related to claims against its former facility in Willowbrook, Illinois.

The company said in a regulatory filing that it entered into a binding term sheet to resolve 97 ethylene oxide claims against Sterigenics related to the facility.

"The settlement includes seven claims that were set for trials in the Circuit Court of Cook County in April and June and the remaining EO claims being pursued by the same plaintiffs’ counsel, including those of 61 claimants with pending lawsuits and 29 claimants who asserted claims but had yet to file new lawsuits," the filing mentioned.

The settlement is subject to several conditions, including 100% participation by the claimants, the sterilization solutions provider said, adding that the company has the right to waive that requirement, in which case the settlement will only be applicable to participating plaintiffs.

Sotera (NASDAQ:SHC) is down 3%, alongside a broader market selloff.

data.title: Dow falls into correction, Nasdaq in bear-market territory as Powell sees 'elevated' risks
data.description:
U.S. stocks fell sharply Friday, losing further ground after Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell pointed to monetary-policy uncertainty in the face of fiscal policies from the Trump administration. 

The Dow (DJI) has dropped into a correction and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) has pushed into bear-market territory as investors navigate sweeping new tariffs the Trump administration this week said it is enacting on imports. China, in retaliation, has slapped a 34% levy on U.S. products. 

"Turning to monetary policy, we face a highly uncertain outlook with elevated risks of both higher unemployment and higher inflation," Powell said in a late-Friday morning speech in Virginia. "The new Administration is in the process of implementing substantial policy changes in four distinct areas: trade, immigration, fiscal policy, and regulation," Powell said.

But "the economy is still in a good place," while uncertainty is high and downside risks have risen, Powell said.

In early afternoon action, the S&P 500 (SP500) was -4.1% at 5,173.60, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -4%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) -3.5%, losing ~1,400 points.

The S&P 500 (SP500) was down nearly 16% from its all-time closing high, and the Dow (DJI) was off ~13% from its record closing high in early December. In Friday trade, the Energy sector (XLE) -7%, leading all 11 groups lower on the market benchmark(SP500). 

The S&P 500(SP500) closed in a correction Thursday, falling -4.8% for its worst session since June 2020.

Market participants did receive a reprieve on Friday in the form of a surprisingly strong March jobs report. 

In the fixed-income market Friday, U.S. Treasury yields slumped as traders flocked to the safety of bonds. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 12 basis points to 3.92%, and the shorter-end 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 16 basis points to 3.55%.

The economic calendar grabbed attention after nonfarm payrolls blew past expectations in March. The unemployment rate edged up to 4.2%. 

"Friday's jobs report shows that the economy is still creating jobs at a robust pace even with the tariff uncertainty and Federal job cuts, and job growth is arguably the most important economic indicator," Glen Smith, chief investment officer at GDS Wealth Management, said. However, the data are "backward looking and doesn't say anything about how employers might fare over the coming months," he said.

data.title: Nike and Lululemon shares jump after Trump's positive chat with Vietnam on tariffs
data.description:
Shares of Nike (NYSE:NKE) and Lululemon (NASDAQ:LULU) took a positive turn on Friday after President Donald Trump posted on his social media platform about a "very productive call" with Vietnam's leaders regarding reciprocal tariffs.

"Just had a very productive call with To Lam, General Secretary of the Communist Party of Vietnam, who told me that Vietnam wants to cut their tariffs down to ZERO if they are able to make an agreement with the U.S. I thanked him on behalf of our country and said I look forward to a meeting in the near future," Trump said in a post Friday on Truth Social.

LULU shares are up 3.4%, and NKE rose 4.5% in reaction to the post by Trump. 

Lululemon and Nike had a majority of their manufacturing moved into Vietnam from China to sidestep the worsening trade relationship between Washington and Beijing. However, during "Liberation Day," Trump imposed a 46% tariff on merchandise imported from the Southeast Asian country.

The reciprocal tariffs are set to take effect on April 9.

data.title: Chip stocks continue free fall as tariff concerns mount; Nasdaq enters bear territory
data.description:
Despite U.S. President Donald Trump's latest tariff announcement exempting semiconductors, shares for these companies continue to skydive, as the Nasdaq Composite Index (COMP:IND) has dropped more than 20% since December, officially entering bear territory, and leaving investors wondering where the bottom is.

Four of the 10 worst performers on a year-to-date basis in the tech-heavy Nasdaq are semiconductor companies. Marvell Technology (NASDAQ:MRVL) is plunging 12% today and is down 50% year to date. ON Semiconductor (NASDAQ:ON) is down 4.5% today and 44% YTD. Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) has fallen 7% today and 38% YTD. Microchip Technology has cascaded 6% today and 34% YTD.

The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index (SOX) sunk 7% by late morning trading. It is at its lowest point since November 2023.

Artificial intelligence chip titan Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) was down another 6%. Since January, the seemingly untouchable GPU producer has nearly wiped out a year's worth of share value gains, and is trading at its lowest since May 2024.

Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU), which was relatively stable during most of the negative moves in this sector over the past two months, was down 9%. It is now down 20% YTD. Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) had declined 7%. Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) was down 5%, and has fallen more than 25% YTD. 

It was reported today that China has responded to Trump's "Liberation Day" tariffs with its own 34% tariffs on all US imports starting on April 10. The 34% levy imposed by China matches the "reciprocal" tariff announced by Trump on Wednesday, which itself added to the existing tariffs on the country. Once the latest U.S. tariffs come into effect on April 9, Chinese goods will face a 54% tax rate.

However, Trump posted on Truth Social, a social media platform owned by Trump Media and Technology Group (DJT), late this morning that his tariff platform has prompted Vietnam to possibly remove tariffs on U.S. goods.  

"Just had a very productive call with To Lam, General Secretary of the Communist Party of Vietnam, who told me that Vietnam wants to cut their Tariffs down to ZERO if they are able to make an agreement with the U.S.," Trump posted. "I thanked him on behalf of our Country, and said I look forward to a meeting in the near future."

Vietnam was hit with a staggering 46% tariff during Trump's Liberation Day announcement.

Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL), which had slipped 4%, produces about 20% of its iPads in Vietnam and assembles much of its wearable products, such as the Apple Watch, there as well, according to Evercore ISI.

And in Indonesia, which received a 32% tariff, Apple recently invested $300M, in order to start selling iPhones there later this month.

India received a 26% tariff. Apple has about 15% of it global iPhone production based in the subcontinent. It is expected to increase to about 25% by the end of the year.

data.title: Safran wins E.U. antitrust OK for Collins Aerospace deal after agreeing to key concession
data.description:
The European Commission said Friday it approved Safran's (OTCPK:SAFRF) (OTCPK:SAFRY) planned $1.8B purchase Collins Aerospace’s flight controls business after winning a key concession, clearing an important regulatory hurdle for the deal to move forward.

The EC said Safran (OTCPK:SAFRF) (OTCPK:SAFRY) offered to divest substantially all of its North American business that focuses on the design and production of trimmable horizontal stabilizer actuator systems, the components in commercial aircraft design that allows for movement of the horizontal tail.

Earlier Friday, the U.K.'s Competition and Markets Authority watchdog said it provisionally found the divestment would address its competition concerns and will ask for feedback from third parties.

Collins Aerospace is a subsidiary of RTX Corp. (NYSE:RTX) and was formed in 2018 from the merger of Rockwell Collins and UTC Aerospace Systems; Collins agreed to sell its actuation and flight controls business unit to Safran in July 2023 for $1.8B.
